title: ACDP Transparency-Log Witness Cosignature (0.4.0)
description: An independent witness's signed observation of an RFC-ACDP-0012 transparency-log checkpoint: 'witness witness_id observed checkpoint (log_id, tree_size, root_hash, timestamp), verified its signature and consistency from its retained head, and cosigned it at witnessed_at' (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4). The cosignature preimage is the JCS canonicalization of this object minus 'signature'; the witness signs the ASCII bytes of 'sha256:<hex(SHA-256(preimage))>' exactly as RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.8 / RFC-ACDP-0010 §5, with the WITNESS's own assertionMethod key (RFC-ACDP-0015 §5) — witnesses are NOT registries and use their own DID and key material. Specified by RFC-ACDP-0015 (acdp/0.4.0), promoting the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.12 reservation; additive minor, so this schema lives in the v0.1.0 namespace (VERSIONING.md).

cosignature_version.description: Cosignature envelope version and in-preimage domain separator (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4; the RFC-ACDP-0011 receipt_version / RFC-ACDP-0012 checkpoint_version convention): a cosignature can never be mistaken for — or replayed as — a checkpoint, a receipt, or any other JCS-canonicalized ACDP object.

witness_id.description: The witness's DID (did:web or did:key). A witness is an independent party, NOT a registry (it has no publish surface); this DID and the key referenced by signature.key_id are the witness's own, never the registry's (RFC-ACDP-0015 §3). The N-witnessed count (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8) is over DISTINCT witness_id values.

log_id.description: The observed log instantiation identifier, copied verbatim from the RFC-ACDP-0012 §6 checkpoint the witness verified.

root_hash.description: The observed checkpoint's root_hash. Together with log_id and tree_size this is the tuple the N-witnessed count is computed over (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8).

timestamp.description: The registry-CLAIMED checkpoint timestamp (the RFC-ACDP-0012 §6 checkpoint.timestamp), copied verbatim. Registry-asserted; the witness does NOT vouch for it — witnessed_at is what the witness attests against parties the registry does not control (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4, §12).

witnessed_checkpoint.description: The identity-bearing subset of the RFC-ACDP-0012 §6 log_checkpoint the witness observed (log_id, tree_size, root_hash, timestamp). Closed: the witness copies exactly these members from the checkpoint it verified; the registry's checkpoint_version and signature are deliberately NOT restated (the consumer verifies the registry checkpoint signature independently, RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3). Honors the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.12 reserved name 'witnessed_checkpoint'.

witnessed_at.description: The witness-clock time at which the witness observed and cosigned the checkpoint; canonical millisecond-precision RFC 3339 UTC (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.3). This is the witness's OWN observation time, anchored against a party the registry does not control — it bounds when the checkpoint existed regardless of the registry's claimed timestamp (RFC-ACDP-0015 §4). Consumers apply a freshness policy to witnessed_at (RFC-ACDP-0015 §7). Honors the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.12 reserved name 'witnessed_at'.

signature.description: The WITNESS's signature over the cosignature hash (RFC-ACDP-0015 §5 — the RFC-ACDP-0010 §5 construction verbatim, but keyed by the witness's own assertionMethod key under the witness's DID). signature.key_id MUST be a DID URL under witness_id.

$comment: Closed schema, as acdp-log-checkpoint.schema.json: every field is signed, so an unknown member would change the preimage. Extensions require a cosignature_version bump. Reserved names from RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.12 adopted: witness_id (the sketch's field), witnessed_checkpoint, witnessed_at. The array member 'witness_signatures' that carries these on checkpoint responses (RFC-ACDP-0015 §6) is a separate top-level envelope member, a sibling of log_inclusion (RFC-ACDP-0012 §10).
